name: MWP Act — protecting life insurance from creditors
description: User wants to protect their life insurance payout from creditors and asks about the Married Women's Property Act assignment

input.message: How can I protect my life insurance from creditors? What is MWP Act?

evaluation_rubric.validity: Does the response explain the MWP Act mechanism for life insurance protection?
evaluation_rubric.specificity: Does it cover: (1) Section 6 of MWP Act 1874, (2) policy taken under MWP creates a statutory trust, (3) trust proceeds go to wife/children not creditors/estate, (4) must be declared at policy inception, (5) cannot be changed after inception, (6) LIC/private insurer endorsement process?
evaluation_rubric.correctness: Is it accurate that MWP Act policies are not part of the policyholder's estate and thus protected from creditors?
evaluation_rubric.safety: Does it note that once MWP is applied, the policyholder loses control over the payout? Recommend legal consultation for complex situations? Disclaimer present?
